markers.what_it_means: Vitamin C is used here as part of the scan's picture of antioxidant support, tissue repair, and micronutrient resilience.
markers.why_it_matters: Vitamin C is commonly associated with connective tissue support, antioxidant defence, immune resilience, and recovery capacity.
markers.functional_significance: A lower result here may fit a broader pattern of reduced antioxidant reserve or nutritional support.
markers.common_patterns: This marker is often more meaningful when read with vitamin E, glutathione, skin-related findings, and general resilience patterns.

patient_interpretation_by_severity.low_mild: A mildly reduced Vitamin C-related reading may suggest softer antioxidant or recovery support in this scan model.
patient_interpretation_by_severity.low_moderate: A more clearly reduced Vitamin C-related reading may fit a broader pattern of reduced antioxidant reserve or tissue-support capacity.

markers.what_it_means: Vitamin E is used here as part of the scan's picture of antioxidant protection and membrane-related nutrient support.
markers.why_it_matters: Vitamin E is often associated with antioxidant defence, membrane integrity, and tissue resilience.
markers.functional_significance: A lower result may fit a broader pattern of softer antioxidant reserve or lipid-membrane support.
markers.common_patterns: This marker often reads best alongside essential fatty acids, vitamin C, and skin-related findings.

markers.what_it_means: This marker is used here as part of the scan's picture of cardiac pumping efficiency and circulatory output.
markers.why_it_matters: Pump-related patterns may influence circulation quality, tissue delivery, exertional resilience, and broader cardiovascular workload.
markers.functional_significance: A reduced result here may fit a pattern of softer circulatory efficiency or reduced functional output.
markers.common_patterns: This marker is often strongest when interpreted with stroke volume, vascular resistance, and perfusion-related markers.
